title: metrosideros macrophylla
intro: Metrosideros macrophylla, also known as the New Zealand Christmas Tree or Pōhutukawa, is a species of flowering tree native to eastern New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are derived from its leaves and bark. The health benefits of Metrosideros macrophylla include the potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and provide antioxidant protection. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support cardiovascular health, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues.
